title: Propagate tenant context across async boundaries
description: A procedure to thread the authenticated tenant through queues, jobs, and downstream calls so scope is never lost.
body: Use when work crosses from a request into a queue, background job, or another service. Do not assume tenant context is implicitly carried across the boundary. Steps: capture the resolved tenant at the edge; embed it in the message or job payload and any downstream call metadata; on the consuming side, re-establish the tenant context before any data access; add a test exercising the full async path. Validation signals include a job and a downstream call operating under the originating tenant. Risk boundary: a dropped tenant context can cause work to touch the wrong tenant's data, so make propagation explicit and verify across every async hop, escalating any gap.
